questions.q: Chandragupta II was an emperor of which dynasty?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta II was a leading emperor of the Gupta dynasty in the late 4th and early 5th centuries CE.

questions.q: Which famous title did Chandragupta II adopt?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta II took the title Vikramaditya, 'Sun of Valour'.

questions.q: Chandragupta II expanded the empire by defeating which rival rulers in western India?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta II conquered the Western Kshatrapas (Shakas) of Malwa, Gujarat and Saurashtra.

questions.q: Chandragupta II ruled during which period?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta II reigned approximately from 375 to 415 CE.

questions.q: Whose son was Chandragupta II?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta II was the son and successor of the great conqueror Samudragupta.

questions.q: Chandragupta II's court is traditionally associated with great poets of which classical language?
questions.explanation: His court is linked with celebrated Sanskrit poets and scholars of the classical golden age.

questions.q: The emblem associated with Chandragupta II in this deck is the?
questions.explanation: The sun emblem reflects his title Vikramaditya, the 'Sun of Valour'.

questions.q: Chandragupta II's victories gave the Guptas access to which coast and its lucrative trade?
questions.explanation: Defeating the Kshatrapas brought the western seaports and maritime trade under Gupta control.

questions.q: Which emperor succeeded Samudragupta and is the subject of this card?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta II Vikramaditya followed Samudragupta on the Gupta throne.

questions.q: What does the title 'Vikramaditya' broadly signify?
questions.explanation: Vikramaditya combines 'vikrama' (valour) and 'aditya' (sun), conveying a king of dazzling prowess.

questions.q: The Iron Pillar of Delhi bears an inscription honouring a king named Chandra, generally identified with whom?
questions.explanation: Most scholars identify the 'Chandra' of the Iron Pillar inscription with Chandragupta II of the Guptas.

questions.q: The Chinese pilgrim who travelled India during Chandragupta II's reign and described its prosperity was?
questions.explanation: Faxian visited India around 399-414 CE, during the reign of Chandragupta II.

questions.q: Tradition holds that Chandragupta II's court included the 'Navaratnas', or nine?
questions.explanation: Legend associates Chandragupta II Vikramaditya with the Navaratnas, the 'nine gems' of learned men at his court.

questions.q: Chandragupta II strengthened ties with the Vakatakas of the Deccan by marrying his daughter to their king; she was named?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta II married his daughter Prabhavatigupta to the Vakataka ruler Rudrasena II, securing a vital southern alliance.

questions.q: Defeating the Western Kshatrapas allowed Chandragupta II to issue what new coinage in conquered regions?
options: Silver coins on the Kshatrapa pattern
questions.explanation: After conquering the Kshatrapas, Chandragupta II struck silver coins modelled on the earlier Kshatrapa issues for western India.

questions.q: Which city in central-western India became an important second centre or capital after Chandragupta II's western conquests?
questions.explanation: Ujjain in Malwa gained prominence as a key Gupta centre following the conquest of the Kshatrapas.

questions.q: Who succeeded Chandragupta II on the Gupta throne?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta II was succeeded by his son Kumaragupta I in the early 5th century CE.

questions.q: The poet often counted among Chandragupta II's 'nine gems' and author of Shakuntala is traditionally said to be?
questions.explanation: Kalidasa is traditionally listed among the Navaratnas of Chandragupta II Vikramaditya's court.

questions.q: Chandragupta II is also known by which alternative personal name in inscriptions and coins?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta II also appears as Devagupta or Devaraja in some sources.

questions.q: The Gupta gold coins of Chandragupta II commonly depict him in which heroic act?
options: Driving a chariot to the Ganges
questions.explanation: Chandragupta II issued a famous Lion-slayer gold type, among other martial coin designs.

questions.q: Faxian's account from Chandragupta II's reign chiefly aimed to gather what?
questions.explanation: Faxian travelled to collect Buddhist texts and visit sacred sites, leaving valuable observations of Gupta society.

questions.q: Chandragupta II's conquest of the Kshatrapas is often dated to around which time?
questions.explanation: The final defeat of the Western Kshatrapas is generally placed around 409 CE, late in Chandragupta II's reign.

questions.q: The Mehrauli Iron Pillar inscription credits king Chandra with a victory in the land of the Vahlikas after crossing which rivers?
options: The five rivers of the Nile
The seven mouths of the Sindhu (Indus)
questions.explanation: The inscription says Chandra crossed the seven mouths of the Sindhu and defeated the Vahlikas, a feat linked to Chandragupta II.

questions.q: After her husband Rudrasena II died, Prabhavatigupta, daughter of Chandragupta II, did what in the Vakataka realm?
options: Founded a new dynasty in Bengal
Led a sea voyage to Java
Ruled as regent for her young sons
questions.explanation: Prabhavatigupta acted as Vakataka regent for her minor sons, extending Gupta influence in the Deccan.

questions.q: A literary tradition (in the play Devichandraguptam) holds that Chandragupta II killed his brother and married his brother's widow, named?
questions.explanation: The tradition relates that Chandragupta II slew his elder brother Ramagupta and married the queen Dhruvadevi, though the account blends history and legend.

questions.q: The astronomer Varahamihira, sometimes linked to the Vikramaditya court tradition, is best known for which work?
questions.explanation: Varahamihira, associated with the Vikramaditya legend, authored the encyclopaedic Brihat Samhita on astronomy and allied sciences.

questions.q: The Udayagiri caves near Vidisha, with inscriptions of Chandragupta II's reign, are famous for a relief of which deity?
questions.explanation: The Udayagiri caves, patronised in Chandragupta II's time, contain a celebrated Varaha (boar incarnation of Vishnu) relief.

questions.q: One of Chandragupta II's ministers, named in the Udayagiri inscription as having helped conquer the whole earth, was?
questions.explanation: The Udayagiri cave inscription records the minister Virasena, who accompanied Chandragupta II on his campaign of conquest.

questions.q: Why is the identification of the Iron Pillar's 'Chandra' with Chandragupta II considered probable but not absolutely certain?
options: The inscription gives a personal name and deeds but no dynastic title or date
questions.explanation: The pillar praises a king 'Chandra' with matching exploits but omits an explicit dynastic title and date, so the identification rests on inference.

questions.q: Chandragupta II's reign is often seen as the political and cultural high point of the Guptas, just before decline began under which later pressures?
questions.explanation: After Chandragupta II and Kumaragupta I, the Huna invasions strained and eventually fragmented the Gupta Empire.
